source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

As VP of Product Design, you’ll lead the creation of Phia’s product experience from the ground up, defining the design vision while scaling the systems, teams, and processes needed to move fast. You’ll operate at the intersection of strategy and execution, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and Leadership to turn ambiguous problems into clear, compelling product experiences. This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who thrives in rapid iteration, isn’t afraid of bold bets, and knows how to build high-quality design at startup speed. You’ll set the bar for craft, velocity, and impact as Phia grows from zero to one and beyond.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




WHAT YOU OWN

 - Own Phia’s product design vision and execution end to end, from zero-to-one to scale

 - Set the bar for product quality, craft, and speed across all user experiences

 - Build, lead, and scale high-impact product design teams in a fast-moving environment

 - Drive design decisions in ambiguous problem spaces and turn ideas into shipped product quickly

 - Partner tightly with Product, Engineering, and Leadership to shape product strategy and priorities

 - Define and evolve design systems that enable rapid iteration and consistent experiences

 - Push bold experimentation while maintaining a cohesive product experience

 - Stay close to the work – reviewing, guiding, and unblocking teams to keep momentum high

 - Use user insights and data to inform decisions and measure impact



Qualifications

 - 10+ years of product design experience, including senior leadership roles in high-growth products

 - Proven experience leading design in zero-to-one or early-scale environments

 - Strong product sense and high bar for craft across interaction, visual, and systems design

 - Track record of building and scaling design teams that ship quickly and well

 - Experience partnering closely with Product and Engineering leadership

 - Comfort operating at speed in ambiguous, evolving environments

 - Clear, direct communicator with strong influence across functions

source_jobs.title: Finance & Strategy Associate
source_jobs.description:
Overview

As a Junior Finance & Strategy Associate at Phia, you'll support the financial and operational backbone of a fast-growing consumer technology platform. Working closely with executives and cross-functional teams, you'll help maintain the systems, models, and processes that drive how the business tracks performance, communicates with investors, and makes decisions. You'll operate at the intersection of finance, strategy, and operations — supporting FP&A and accounting while also serving as a reliable partner to internal teams navigating day-to-day business needs. This is a hands-on, early-career role designed for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, takes ownership naturally, and wants to build a strong foundation in finance and business operations at a scaling startup.




About Phia

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




Who You Are

 - Detail-Oriented: The small stuff matters to you, not because someone told you it does, but because you genuinely care about getting things right. You read things twice, double-check your work, and take quiet pride in delivering something clean.

 - Reliable + Hustle: You show up, follow through, and don't need reminders. People know that when you're on something, it's handled. You bring energy to the work without making a big deal out of it.

 - Entrepreneurship + Autopilot: You're naturally curious about how things work and why decisions get made. When something's unclear, you figure it out rather than wait for someone to hand you the answer. You're comfortable taking initiative and find it satisfying when you can make something run a little smoother.




What You Own

Finance (30%)

 - Assist with FP&A work by maintaining financial models, pulling monthly and quarterly reports, and helping turn data into clear summaries for the team, including historical analysis and budget forecasting.

 - Support the tracking and analysis of business and financial metrics across revenue, growth, and operations on a weekly, monthly, and quarterly basis, surfacing insights and trends to support decision-making.

 - Help prepare investor materials and update quarterly reports, ensuring everything is accurate, clean, and presentation-ready.

 - Help streamline reporting processes by building more automated and scalable solutions, including leveraging AI tools to reduce manual work.

Operation (70%)

 - Help field cross-departmental operational requests by finding the right information and following through until things are taken care of.

 - Support the tracking and maintenance of company OKRs, keeping progress up to date and surfacing insights to the leadership team.

 - Take on ad-hoc projects across the business and run with them indep

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

As a Senior Backend Engineer, you’ll design, build, and scale core backend systems that power high-traffic consumer products and internal platforms. You’ll own foundational services and APIs, work on high-throughput distributed systems, and partner closely with product, ML, and engineering teams to deliver reliable, low-latency experiences at scale.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




WHAT YOU OWN

 - Design and build scalable backend services, APIs, and shared platform components

 - Own system architecture, performance, and reliability for core backend systems

 - Partner with product and ML teams to support data- and model-driven workflows

 - Improve observability, monitoring, and operational best practices

 - Drive technical decisions and contribute to long-term platform strategy

 - Raise the quality bar through code reviews, design discussions, and mentorship




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience building and operating backend systems in production

 - Strong experience with APIs, databases, and distributed systems

 - Proficiency in at least one backend language and modern backend frameworks

 - Experience designing systems for scale, performance, and reliability

 - Strong ownership mindset and ability to work in fast-moving environments

 - Clear communicator who collaborates well across functions

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer Intern
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

As a Full Stack Engineering Intern at Phia, you will help build and ship product features that power our creator and brand ecosystem. You will work across the stack to develop user-facing experiences, support product data systems, and improve the internal tools that enable content publishing and performance insights. Partnering closely with engineers, product managers, and designers, you will contribute to real projects that directly impact our community. This internship offers hands-on experience building scalable systems in a fast-moving startup environment while learning engineering best practices from a high-performing team.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




WHAT YOU OWN

 - Contribute to frontend and backend features for user-facing products

 - Support the development of APIs and data flows that power content publishing, product data, and analytics workflows

 - Partner with designers, product managers, and engineers to ship end-to-end features

 - Learn and apply best practices for building scalable and maintainable software in a production environment




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently pursuing a degree in Computer Science or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience

 - Experience through coursework, internships, or personal projects building web applications

 - Proficiency in JavaScript and experience with at least one frontend and one backend framework

 - Foundational understanding of APIs, databases, and web application architecture

 - Familiarity with Git, testing practices, and modern development workflows

 - Strong problem-solving skills, curiosity, and ability to learn quickly in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Clear communicator who enjoys collaborating and taking ownership of their work

source_jobs.title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

As a Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Phia, you’ll build and scale production ML systems that power core product experiences and decision-making. You’ll work across the full ML stack, from data and modeling to deployment and iteration, on problems like ranking, personalization, and optimization. This role sits at the intersection of machine learning, product engineering, and data platforms, with ownership over systems that directly impact growth and user experience. You’ll ship models to production, run experiments at speed, and help define how machine learning is done as Phia scales.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




WHAT YOU OWN

 - Design, develop, and deploy production machine learning models that power core product experiences and decision-making systems

 - Own the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle, including data analysis, feature engineering, model training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring

 - Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Data, and Operations to translate product requirements and business goals into scalable ML solutions

 - Develop experimentation frameworks and causal measurement strategies to evaluate model impact and inform product decisions

 - Build and maintain forecasting, ranking, personalization, or optimization systems operating at scale

 - Drive improvements to model performance, reliability, and scalability in production environments

 - Contribute to the ML platform and infrastructure, improving tooling for training, experimentation, and monitoring

 - Influence technical direction through design reviews, code reviews, and mentorship of other engineers
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of industry experience building and deploying machine learning systems in production

 - Strong proficiency in Python and experience with common ML frameworks and libraries (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost, LightGBM, scikit-learn)

 - Experience owning the full ML lifecycle, from data exploration to production deployment and iteration

 - Experience working with large-scale, real-world datasets and noisy or incomplete data

 - Solid understanding of experiment design and causal inference, including A/B testing and offline evaluation

 - Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners and communicate technical concepts clearly

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

As a Senior Product Manager at Phia, you'll help define and build the future of AI-powered shopping. You'll own major product initiatives from strategy through execution, working closely with Engineering, Design, Data, and Leadership to identify opportunities, solve complex user problems, and deliver experiences that drive meaningful business impact.

This role is equal parts strategic and hands-on. You'll be responsible for shaping product direction, making thoughtful prioritization decisions, and leading cross-functional teams to ship high-quality products at startup speed. You'll work directly with company leadership and have significant influence over what we build, why we build it, and how we measure success.

We're looking for someone who combines strong product intuition with analytical rigor, thrives in fast-moving environments, and is excited to help build a category-defining consumer AI company.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




WHAT YOU OWN

 - Own product strategy, roadmap, and execution across key areas of the Phia platform

 - Identify opportunities through user research, product data, market insights, and business priorities

 - Partner closely with Engineering and Design to define, prioritize, and deliver high-impact product initiatives

 - Lead products from concept to launch, balancing user needs, technical complexity, and business goals

 - Define success metrics and use experimentation, analytics, and user feedback to guide decision-making

 - Drive alignment across stakeholders and communicate product priorities, tradeoffs, and outcomes

 - Help establish and scale product processes as the company continues to grow

 - Mentor and influence product thinking across teams




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of product management experience, preferably building consumer-facing software products

 - Proven track record of leading cross-functional teams and shipping successful products from concept to launch

 - Strong product judgment and ability to turn ambiguous problems into clear, actionable plans

 - Experience using data to inform decisions and measure product impact

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to influence stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with a high degree of ownership

source_jobs.title: AI Agents Manager, Brand Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

Phia is looking for a Brand Intelligences & AI Agents Manager to own and scale the brand relationships that power our commerce ecosystem.

You will manage thousands of brand partnerships across fashion, beauty, and home. Your job is to build the systems, automations, workflows, and communication infrastructure that allow Phia to manage brand relationships at scale.

You will negotiate commission rate increases, identify growth opportunities, surface the right brands for editorial moments, automate reporting, improve CRM hygiene, and create the operating system that helps our partnerships team move faster. You will work directly with our editorial, data, and partnerships teams to make sure the right brands are promoted to the right shoppers at the right time.

This role is ideal for someone who loves brands, commerce, and fashion, but also thinks like a systems builder. You should be the kind of person who sees a repetitive workflow and immediately asks, “How can I automate this?”




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43.5M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In under a year, Phia’s consumer shopping agent has surpassed one million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. Each month, Phia drives millions of dollars in sales for brands and has achieved 9 figures in sales growth since launch.

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user’s intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship quickly, own meaningful systems, and solve complex problems across consumer AI, commerce, and brand partnerships, this is the place to do it.

 
 


WHAT YOU OWN

 - Own and scale a portfolio of thousands of brand relationships across fashion, beauty, and home.

 - Build the systems that make brand relationship management scalable, including automated reporting, CRM workflows, brand communication templates, performance dashboards, and follow-up systems.

 - Proactively negotiate commission rate increases with brand partners, using performance data and a clear point of view on the value Phia drives.

 - Partner closely with editorial to identify which brands should be featured in key shopping moments, campaigns, and trend-driven editorials.

 - Translate brand priorities, commission rates, merchandising calendars, and performance data into clear internal recommendations.

 - Build and maintain automations around brand performance tracking, relationship touchpoints, CRM hygiene, campaign sourcing, and partner follow-up.

 - Monitor brand performance and identify trends, risks, and opportunities before they become obvious.

 - Source new brands and outbound campaign opportunities for the partnerships team.

 - Help turn strong brand partners into long-term Phia advocates by communicating clearly, moving quickly, and consistently creating value.

 - Represent Phia to brand partners with confidence, warmth, urgency, and authority.
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1 to 5 years of experience in partnerships, revenue operations, affiliate, AI agents, account management, brand partnerships, e-commerce, media, or a high-growth startup.

 - You are excited by the idea of managing thousands of relationships through systems, automation, and leverage.

 - You are fluent with AI tools and automation platforms. You are comfortable using tools like Claude, ChatGPT, Zapier, Make, Airtable, HubSpot, Notion, Google Sheets, or similar platforms to automate repetitive work and improve team workflows.

 - You are a strong negotiator. You are comfortable asking for more, ba

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

As a Full Stack Engineer at Phia, you’ll build and ship features across frontend and backend systems that power our creator and brand ecosystem. You’ll work on content publishing, product data, and performance tooling—learning by shipping real product, partnering closely with experienced engineers, and contributing to platforms that scale with the company.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.




WHAT YOU OWN

 - Build and maintain frontend and backend features for internal and external tools

 - Implement APIs and data flows that support content, product, and analytics workflows

 - Collaborate with designers, product managers, and engineers to ship end-to-end features

 - Debug and improve existing systems with a focus on reliability and performance

 - Contribute to code quality, testing, and documentation

 - Learn and apply best practices for scalable, maintainable software




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1+ years of experience (or equivalent internships, projects, or coursework) in software development

 - Experience with JavaScript and at least one frontend and one backend framework

 - Basic understanding of APIs, databases, and web application architecture

 - Familiarity with version control, testing, and modern development workflows

 - Strong problem-solving skills and willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment

 - Clear communicator with a collaborative, ownership-oriented mindset

source_jobs.title: Pitch Yourself
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

We’re looking for exceptional people who want to build something meaningful, move fast, and take real ownership from day one. This role is designed for individuals who are eager to learn, comfortable with ambiguity, and motivated by impact—not titles or rigid job descriptions.

At Phia, you’ll work on problems that matter, alongside a small, high-performing team operating at startup speed. You’ll contribute directly to the growth and quality of a product used by millions, with the opportunity to shape how things are built, improved, and scaled. The exact scope of your role will evolve based on your strengths, interests, and where you can have the biggest impact.

If you’re someone who takes initiative, learns quickly, and wants to grow in an environment with high trust and high expectations, we want to hear from you.




ABOUT PHIA

Phia has raised $43M from Notable Capital, Khosla Ventures, and Kleiner Perkins, with backing from founders and operators like Vlad Tenev (Robinhood), Mellody Hobson (Ariel Investments), Naomi Gleit (Meta), and Mati Staniszewski (ElevenLabs), plus a roster of cultural leaders, to build the AI alignment layer for commerce. In just over a year, Phia's consumer shopping agent has surpassed 1.5 million users and partnered with 9,600+ retail brands across contemporary, resale, and luxury, representing billions in annual gross merchandise volume. We scan more than 350 million products to help shoppers find the right pieces at the best price, cutting return rates by 50%, and we're on pace for nine-figure sales growth this year.

 

In an era where AI vertical agents are reshaping every industry, commerce is on the verge of a complete transformation. Phia is reinventing shopping from a fragmented, impersonal experience into one that feels intelligent, trusted, and built around each user's intent. This foundation of trust is our wedge to become the end-to-end shopping destination for the next generation of buyers.

 

Phia is a lean, high-ownership team building at startup speed. If you want to ship at high velocity and solve complex problems in consumer AI and commerce, this is the place to do it.

notes: Phia is a consumer technology platform with a strong focus on product and engineering. The company has raised $43M in funding and is backed by notable investors.
